questions.question: According to AFI 1-1, which members must have a family care plan?
options: All USAF members regardless of family status
Single-member parents with custody of children who bear sole or joint responsibility, and military couples with dependents
Only dual-military couples with dependents under age 5
Only members assigned to overseas duty stations with dependents

questions.question: How quickly must members notify their commander or first sergeant of changes in personal status or family circumstances that require completion of a DAF Form 357?
options: Immediately, or within 30 days (60 days for Selected Reserve)

questions.question: What is the consequence of failing to produce a family care plan within 60 days of discussion with the commander, supervisor, or commander's designated representative?
options: Mandatory reassignment to a non-deployable position

questions.question: What form is used for Family Care Certification?
How often are commanders or first sergeants required to brief military members with family care responsibilities?
VA disability compensation is graduated according to the degree of the veteran's disability. What is the scale range and increment?
options: 5 percent to 100 percent in increments of 5 percent
10 percent to 100 percent in increments of 10 percent
10 percent to 100 percent in increments of 5 percent
20 percent to 100 percent in increments of 10 percent

questions.question: At what combined disability rating may a veteran with dependents receive an additional allowance for VA disability compensation?
Which health care program serves uniformed service members, retirees, their family members, survivors, and some former spouses?
What are the two primary health care plan options offered to eligible TRICARE beneficiaries?
options: TRICARE For Life and TRICARE Young Adult

questions.question: How many years of Total Active Federal Military Service (TAFMS) must enlisted members have to be eligible for retirement?
In addition to 20 years of TAFMS, how many years of total active federal commissioned service must officers have to be eligible to retire?
A retirement application can typically be submitted through myFSS up to how many months before the desired retirement date, but no less than how many months?
options: Up to 6 months, but no less than 2 months
Up to 12 months, but no less than 4 months
Up to 18 months, but no less than 6 months
Up to 24 months, but no less than 12 months

questions.question: Under the Blended Retirement System (BRS), what is the retirement pay multiplier per year of service?
Under the BRS, when does the DoD begin an automatic contribution of one percent of base pay to the Thrift Savings Plan?
Under the BRS, the DoD begins matching contributions up to an additional four percent of base pay after how many years of service?
The Survivor Benefit Plan annuity payable is what percent of the retired pay the member would have been entitled to receive?
Under the Survivor Benefit Plan 'paid-up' feature, at what age and after how many months of premium payments can a member stop paying premiums while remaining an active participant?
When can a Survivor Benefit Plan participant terminate coverage?
options: At any time after beginning receipt of retired pay
During a one-year window between the second and third anniversary following the date beginning receipt of retired pay
During a six-month window at the fifth anniversary of retirement

questions.question: What is the toll-free number for service members and families in the United States to reach the American Red Cross?
How many days of leave do members accrue for each month of RegAF status?
What is the maximum number of leave days a member may accumulate by the end of a fiscal year?
How many weeks of non-chargeable leave does the Military Parental Leave Program authorize for covered service members after a qualifying birth event or adoption?
Bereavement Leave provides up to how many days of non-chargeable paid leave in connection with the death of a member's spouse or child?
To be eligible for bereavement leave, a member must have less than how many days of accrued ordinary leave on the date of the death?
Emergency Leave of Absence (non-chargeable leave) allows commanders to grant up to how many days, and how many times during a member's career?
options: Up to 7 days, once during a career
Up to 14 days, once during an entire career
Up to 21 days, twice during a career
Up to 30 days, once during a career

questions.question: Under the We Are All Recruiters (WEAR) program, applicants are defined as individuals within what age range?
How many days of sick leave do civilian employees earn each year?
What is the annual leave accumulation cap for most civilian employees, and what is the cap for those working overseas?
options: 30 days for most; 45 days for overseas
45 days for most; 60 days for overseas
30 days for most; 60 days for overseas
60 days for most; 90 days for overseas

questions.question: Approximately what percent of USAF civilian employees are covered by labor agreements between unions and installations or major commands?
Under the CSB/REDUX retirement option, what is the value of each of the first 20 years of service toward the retirement multiplier?
Which VA educational benefit programs are listed as available to service members and veterans?
options: Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery GI Bill, Reserve Educational Assistance Program, and Survivors' and Dependents' Educational Assistance
Post-9/11 GI Bill, Federal Tuition Assistance, and Vocational Rehabilitation
Montgomery GI Bill, Federal Pell Grant, and Yellow Ribbon Program only
GI Bill, Federal Stafford Loan Program, and Military Spouse Education Assistance

questions.question: How many national cemeteries does the VA National Cemetery Administration operate to provide burial space for eligible individuals?
What is Dependency and Indemnity Compensation?
options: A taxable monthly payment to surviving spouses only
A one-time lump sum payment to veterans with service-connected disabilities
A tax-free monetary benefit generally payable to a surviving spouse, child, or parent of service members or survivors of veterans who died from service-connected disabilities
A supplemental retirement payment for veterans rated at 50 percent or higher disability

questions.question: How many dental plans does TRICARE offer?
Under the Blended Retirement System, Airmen who elect a lump sum payment of a portion of their retirement pay may receive it in up to how many annual installments?
What is the minimum base amount a service member can elect as the basis of Survivor Benefit Plan coverage?
What automated system is used for requesting and processing military leave, and what form serves as a backup when that system is unavailable?
What happens to a member's entitlement to pay, allowances, and leave accrual when they enter excess leave status?
options: Pay and allowances continue at half rate, but leave accrual stops
Pay, allowances, and leave accrual all continue as normal
Entitlement to pay and allowances and leave accrual stops on the first day of excess leave
Pay continues but allowances and leave accrual are suspended for 30 days

questions.question: How many steps are in each General Schedule grade for civilian employees?
What is the normal regular tour of duty for most civilian employees?
options: Four ten-hour days, Monday through Thursday
Five eight-hour days, Monday through Friday
Six six-hour days, Monday through Saturday
Five nine-hour days with alternating Fridays off
